SEARCH
Package com.orasaka.core.config

Class OrasakaCoreConfiguration

java.lang.Object
com.orasaka.core.config.OrasakaCoreConfiguration
@Configuration public class OrasakaCoreConfiguration extends Object
Spring Configuration that wires environment-driven AI provider models into the Orasaka engine and registers all core service beans.

All model instances (ChatModel, EmbeddingModel, ImageModel, TextToSpeechModel) are built dynamically from the CoreProperties record, which itself is populated from application.yml via Environment bindings. No model name, API key, or base URL is hardcoded — all are externalized (AGENTS.md §1.E).

Provider maps are keyed by the provider name string (e.g., "ollama", "openai") and passed to the OrasakaEngine constructor which selects the active provider at runtime based on CoreProperties.defaultProvider().

See Also:
  • CoreProperties
  • OrasakaEngine
  • AbstractOrasakaEngine

  • Constructor Details

    • OrasakaCoreConfiguration

      public OrasakaCoreConfiguration()

  • Method Details

    • orasakaEngine

      @Bean public OrasakaEngine orasakaEngine(CoreProperties properties, OrasakaToolRegistry toolRegistry, OrasakaKnowledgeService knowledgeService, McpOrchestrator mcpOrchestrator, OrasakaMemoryResolver memoryResolver, Map<String,org.springframework.ai.chat.model.ChatModel> chatModels, Map<String,org.springframework.ai.image.ImageModel> imageModels, Map<String,org.springframework.ai.embedding.EmbeddingModel> embeddingModels, Map<String,org.springframework.ai.audio.tts.TextToSpeechModel> speechModels)
      Declares the primary OrasakaEngine bean, injecting all resolved model maps and orchestration services.

      The engine is the central orchestrator for RAG, MCP, tool attachment, and AI inference. It is exposed externally only through the OrasakaAiClient facade (Bridge Pattern 2.0 — AGENTS.md §1.B).

      Parameters:
      properties - Externalized configuration for provider selection and overrides.
      toolRegistry - Registry of native Java tools available for AI function calling.
      knowledgeService - RAG knowledge retrieval service backed by a VectorStore.
      mcpOrchestrator - Bridge for external MCP server context and tool resolution.
      memoryResolver - Session-scoped conversation memory resolver.
      chatModels - Map of provider-key → ChatModel implementations.
      imageModels - Map of provider-key → ImageModel implementations.
      embeddingModels - Map of provider-key → EmbeddingModel implementations.
      speechModels - Map of provider-key → TextToSpeechModel implementations.
      Returns:
      A fully initialized OrasakaEngine ready for Virtual Thread execution.
      See Also:
      • OrasakaAiClient

    • chatModels

      @Bean public Map<String,org.springframework.ai.chat.model.ChatModel> chatModels(CoreProperties properties)
      Builds the provider-keyed map of ChatModel instances from configuration.

      Currently supported providers:

      • ollama — configured via orasaka.core.overrides.ollama. Defaults to http://localhost:11434 and model llama3:8b if not overridden.
      • openai — configured via orasaka.core.overrides.openai. Requires OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable; falls back to a dummy key for local runs.
      Providers not present in CoreProperties.overrides() are silently omitted from the map.
      Parameters:
      properties - The resolved core configuration record.
      Returns:
      A Map of provider names to their ChatModel implementations.
      See Also:
      • CoreProperties.ProviderConfig

    • embeddingModels

      @Bean public Map<String,org.springframework.ai.embedding.EmbeddingModel> embeddingModels(CoreProperties properties)
      Builds the provider-keyed map of EmbeddingModel instances from configuration.

      Currently, only the ollama provider is wired for embedding. The embedding model name is resolved from orasaka.core.overrides.ollama.extra.embedding-model, defaulting to all-minilm per the local resource constraints directive (AGENTS.md §1.D).

      Parameters:
      properties - The resolved core configuration record.
      Returns:
      A Map of provider names to their EmbeddingModel implementations.
      See Also:
      • CoreProperties.ProviderConfig.extra()

    • imageModels

      @Bean public Map<String,org.springframework.ai.image.ImageModel> imageModels(CoreProperties properties)
      Builds the provider-keyed map of ImageModel instances from configuration.

      Currently, only the openai provider supports image generation via Spring AI 1.1.6. Ollama does not expose a dedicated image generation model in this version.

      Parameters:
      properties - The resolved core configuration record.
      Returns:
      A Map of provider names to their ImageModel implementations.

    • speechModels

      @Bean public Map<String,org.springframework.ai.audio.tts.TextToSpeechModel> speechModels()
      Provides an empty TextToSpeechModel map as a placeholder.

      TTS provider wiring is deferred until a supported Spring AI 1.1.6-compatible TTS implementation is available. Voice preference resolution from OrasakaContext is already implemented in AbstractOrasakaEngine and will activate automatically once a provider is registered here.

      Returns:
      An empty mutable Map, to be populated by future provider beans.

    • coreProperties

      @Bean public CoreProperties coreProperties(org.springframework.core.env.Environment env)
      Constructs the CoreProperties record by binding values from the Spring Environment.

      This manual binding approach is required because orasaka-core must not declare spring-boot-starter as a dependency (AGENTS.md §1.A). The Environment is injected by the host application's Spring context and provides access to all application.yml properties and environment variable overrides.

      Property prefix: orasaka.core.*. All values support environment variable substitution via the standard ${ENV_VAR:default} Spring syntax in the consuming application's YAML.

      Parameters:
      env - The Spring Environment provided by the host application context.
      Returns:
      A fully initialized, immutable CoreProperties record.
      See Also:
      • CoreProperties
